title: Libya
key_findings: Government-linked and independent militias operate across the country, kidnapping and detaining Christian migrants and suspected converts and harassing members of disfavored Muslim communities, such as Sufi and Ibadi Muslims.

Wolf Freedom of Religion or Belief Victims List Country Profiles Defending Freedoms Project Publications " class="we-megamenu-nolink"> Annual Reports Contact Us Facebook Twitter YouTube Instagram Search Libya View the Libya chapters in our annual reports > Recent News Events Publications Advising Government Religious freedom conditions in Libya have significantly deteriorated amid the country’s political fragmentation, as the Tripoli-based government detains and prosecutes those it suspects of atheism, apostasy from Islam, and proselytization for Christianity. Government-linked and independent militias operate across the country, kidnapping and detaining Christian migrants and suspected converts and harassing members of disfavored Muslim communities, such as Sufi and Ibadi Muslims.
